chunks.topic: profile backend systems ai judge
chunks.text: Akash Tomy is a backend-focused full stack developer from Alappuzha, Kerala, India. He works with Django, FastAPI, Redis, Celery, relational databases, AWS, and React. His portfolio now emphasizes backend systems thinking: race conditions, cache invalidation, transactional integrity, query optimization, async workers, and AI evaluation architecture. He is actively looking for full-time backend, full-stack, Django, or AI infrastructure roles.

chunks.topic: contact
chunks.text: Akash Tomy's contact details: Email: akashtomy174@gmail.com, Phone: +91 9497634775, Location: Alappuzha, Kerala, India. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/akash-tomy-8b51a737b/, GitHub: https://github.com/AkashTomy174, Portfolio: https://akashtomy.com/, EasyBuy live demo: https://easybuy.akashtomy.com/.

chunks.topic: availability roles
chunks.text: Akash Tomy is based in Alappuzha, Kerala, India. He is open to full-time roles, remote or on-site within India. He is especially interested in backend engineering, Django development, full-stack product engineering, and AI infrastructure work.

chunks.topic: education
chunks.text: Akash Tomy completed his Bachelor of Computer Application (BCA) from Christ College Pulliyanmala, Kattapana, Idukki, Kerala from 2022 to 2025. His CGPA is 7.48. Relevant coursework includes Data Structures, DBMS, Cloud Computing Basics, and Design and Analysis of Algorithms.

chunks.topic: internship django react mysql aws
chunks.text: Akash Tomy worked as a Python Full Stack Developer Intern at SMEC Technologies from July 2024 to present. He reduced page-load database queries on high-traffic catalog and auth pages by adding Redis caching for catalog listings, header data, wishlist state, and auth-related lookups. He developed reusable React components across multiple views, designed normalized relational schemas in MySQL with foreign key constraints and indexing, deployed a Django application to AWS EC2 with Nginx and Gunicorn, and participated in daily code reviews using feature branch workflow and pull requests.

chunks.topic: ai project judge flagship evaluation system
chunks.text: AI Project Judge is Akash's upcoming flagship engineering project. It is designed as an AI-assisted repository evaluation system for hackathons, portfolios, and hiring screens. The goal is not to ask an LLM to guess a score from a repo. The system combines deterministic static analysis, repository ingestion, token budgeting, project-type classification, and LLM interpretation to produce more grounded, auditable evaluations.

chunks.topic: projects portfolio overview easybuy ai project judge ai akash
chunks.text: Akash's highlighted projects are EasyBuy, AI Project Judge, and AI Akash. EasyBuy is a deployed full-stack e-commerce platform with production backend work around caching, payments, concurrency, and AWS deployment. AI Project Judge is an architecture-first repository evaluation system in progress. AI Akash is the portfolio chatbot built with FastAPI, Gemini, ChromaDB retrieval, caching, rate limiting, and optional voice output.

chunks.topic: ai judge ingestion token budget github monorepo
chunks.text: The AI Project Judge ingestion plan handles GitHub repositories by detecting languages, filtering dependency folders and generated noise, ranking important files, chunking large files, and capping model context around a 15k token budget. This is meant to keep large monorepos from exploding cost or pushing irrelevant files into the evaluation prompt.

chunks.topic: ai judge static analysis deterministic scoring
chunks.text: AI Project Judge uses deterministic static checks before AI scoring so the model is grounded in facts. Planned signals include tests and coverage with pytest-cov, linting with Ruff or ESLint, complexity with Radon, security checks with Safety or npm audit, type checks with mypy or TypeScript, dependency health, CI presence, and README quality.

chunks.topic: ai judge llm scoring rubric prompt orchestration
chunks.text: The AI layer in AI Project Judge is planned to interpret the static metrics and selected source snippets against a scoring rubric. It should evaluate code quality, maintainability, testing, security, architecture, and documentation in parallel criteria. The prompt should cite the evidence it used instead of giving vague praise.

chunks.topic: ai judge architecture fastapi celery redis postgres sse
chunks.text: AI Project Judge is planned around a FastAPI backend, Celery workers for long-running repository evaluation tasks, Redis as the broker/cache, PostgreSQL for persisted evaluations, GitHub API ingestion, server-sent events for live job state, and a React dashboard with radar charts, logs, and downloadable JSON or PDF reports.

chunks.topic: ai judge cost optimization reliability caching retries
chunks.text: AI Project Judge treats LLM cost and reliability as design constraints. Planned optimizations include ranked file selection, token caps, prompt caching, deterministic pre-scoring, retry handling, status transitions, and avoiding synchronous HTTP requests for long repo evaluations. Rough planning estimates compared a standard batch around Rs 3700 for 300 repos with an optimized path closer to Rs 950.

chunks.topic: ai judge roadmap status
chunks.text: AI Project Judge is currently an architecture-first project and build roadmap, not a fully shipped production product yet. Akash is presenting it as a flagship systems direction because the interesting work is in the engineering decisions: ingestion, static analysis, token budgeting, queue processing, auditability, and cost control.

chunks.topic: portfolio chatbot rag fastapi gemini retrieval cache
chunks.text: The AI Akash portfolio chatbot is a FastAPI backend connected to a React widget. It validates chat requests, rate limits by IP, caches repeated answers with an LRU cache, retrieves curated knowledge chunks with Gemini embeddings plus keyword and topic overlap, stores vectors in ChromaDB, sends only retrieved context to a guarded Gemini prompt, and can optionally generate ElevenLabs voice audio.

chunks.topic: portfolio chatbot next version embeddings vector search citations
chunks.text: The next version of the AI Akash chatbot should add chunk versioning, stronger source citations, and a regression prompt set to catch bad answers. That would make the chatbot feel less like a portfolio accessory and more like a small RAG system with measurable behavior.

chunks.topic: easybuy ecommerce django react aws
chunks.text: EasyBuy is Akash Tomy's featured full-stack e-commerce platform with Customer, Seller, and Admin roles. It is live at https://easybuy.akashtomy.com/ and the source code is at https://github.com/AkashTomy174/easybuy. It is built with Python, Django, React.js, Tailwind CSS, MySQL, AWS EC2/RDS/S3, Redis, Celery, Razorpay, GitHub Actions, Cloudflare Workers, and OpenAI API.

chunks.topic: easybuy performance redis query optimization n+1
chunks.text: In EasyBuy, Akash reduced database queries by 42%, from 36 queries to 21, using Redis caching and Django ORM optimization with select_related and prefetch_related. Celery async workers handle notifications and email delivery with a DB fallback, keeping order responses under 300ms by moving slow work out of the request path.

chunks.topic: easybuy payments concurrency select_for_update overselling
chunks.text: EasyBuy handles payments with Razorpay. Akash designed a concurrent-safe payment system using database locking with select_for_update to prevent duplicate transactions. He also prevented overselling under concurrent load using atomic DB transactions and select_for_update, ensuring inventory accuracy across simultaneous orders. HMAC webhook signature verification protects payment callbacks across all 3 user roles.

chunks.topic: easybuy ai chatbot openai catalog context
chunks.text: EasyBuy includes a product-aware AI chatbot built using the OpenAI API and Cloudflare Workers. Live catalog context is injected per request so the chatbot has up-to-date product information when answering customer questions.

chunks.topic: easybuy deployment aws nginx gunicorn ci cd
chunks.text: EasyBuy is deployed on AWS EC2 with automated CI/CD via GitHub Actions. Zero-downtime reloads are achieved using Gunicorn and Nginx with pre-flight migration checks before each deployment. The database runs on Amazon RDS and static files are served from AWS S3 via Cloudflare CDN.

chunks.topic: backend django fastapi redis celery payments
chunks.text: Akash's backend skills include Python, Django, Django REST Framework, FastAPI, REST APIs, WebSockets, Celery async workers, JWT authentication, OAuth 2.0 with Google, Razorpay payment integration with HMAC webhooks, OpenAI or Gemini API integration, concurrency control with select_for_update, and atomic transactions.

chunks.topic: frontend react tailwind vite framer motion
chunks.text: Akash's frontend skills include React.js with hooks and context, JavaScript ES6+, Tailwind CSS, HTML5, and CSS3. His portfolio is built with React, Tailwind CSS, Vite, and Framer Motion.

chunks.topic: cloud devops aws linux nginx gunicorn github actions
chunks.text: Akash works with AWS EC2, S3, and RDS, Linux Ubuntu, Nginx, Gunicorn, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Cloudflare CDN, and Cloudflare Workers. EasyBuy uses CI/CD through GitHub Actions to AWS EC2 with Gunicorn and Nginx.

chunks.topic: database cache mysql postgres redis orm locking
chunks.text: Akash works with MySQL, Amazon RDS, Redis caching, Django ORM optimization using prefetch_related and select_related, database locking with select_for_update, and atomic transactions. He thinks about TTLs, stale cache risk, N+1 queries, and what should not be cached.

chunks.topic: tools git github postman linux reviews
chunks.text: Akash's tools include Git, GitHub, Postman, Linux Ubuntu, and feature branch workflow with pull requests. He maintains clean Git history and participates in code reviews.

chunks.topic: ai tools gemini openai chromadb elevenlabs
chunks.text: Akash has worked with AI tooling including Gemini API, OpenAI API, ChromaDB for vector retrieval, and ElevenLabs for optional voice output. His portfolio chatbot uses Gemini for generation and embeddings, ChromaDB for retrieval, and ElevenLabs when voice mode is enabled.

chunks.topic: languages
chunks.text: Akash Tomy speaks English, Malayalam, Hindi, and Tamil.

chunks.topic: recruiter fit different
chunks.text: What stands out about Akash is that he thinks past the happy path. He pays attention to things like safe payments under concurrency, measurable query reduction, async work, zero-downtime deployment, token budgeting, and the tradeoffs behind caching and consistency. He is not just trying to make features work; he is trying to make them hold up in production.

chunks.topic: strengths backend systems
chunks.text: Akash's strongest area is backend engineering with systems awareness. He is comfortable designing Django and FastAPI APIs, tightening ORM queries, using Redis and Celery, handling payments safely under concurrency, deploying on AWS, and thinking through AI systems with cost and reliability in mind.

chunks.topic: experience
chunks.text: Akash has been building with Django and React since 2022. Since July 2024, he has been working as a Python Full Stack Developer Intern at SMEC Technologies. He also independently built and deployed EasyBuy on AWS and is now developing AI Project Judge as a systems-heavy AI evaluation pipeline.

chunks.topic: hire recruiter fit backend
chunks.text: Akash is worth considering because he already has hands-on production experience from EasyBuy and a clear systems direction through AI Project Judge. He can speak concretely about query reduction, Redis caching, stale cache risk, select_for_update, payment safety, async workers, token budgeting, static analysis, and deployment. He learns quickly and is actively looking for full-time backend or full-stack roles.

chunks.topic: project count only one project portfolio breadth
chunks.text: Akash's portfolio highlights multiple projects, not just one: EasyBuy, a deployed full-stack e-commerce platform with production backend work around caching, payments, concurrency, and AWS deployment; AI Project Judge, an architecture-first repository evaluation system in progress; and AI Akash, the portfolio chatbot itself. EasyBuy is the shipped flagship product, while AI Project Judge shows his systems thinking and AI infrastructure direction.

chunks.topic: availability
chunks.text: Akash is actively looking for full-time opportunities and can discuss availability directly. The easiest way to reach him is by email at akashtomy174@gmail.com or on LinkedIn at https://www.linkedin.com/in/akash-tomy-8b51a737b/.
